title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast!
longTailKeywords: how to make butter chicken at home
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy, and flavorful Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a beloved Indian dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian curry known for its tender chicken pieces simmered in a luscious tomato-based sauce. This Murgh Makhani recipe delivers an authentic taste experience, perfect for a comforting and satisfying meal. Enjoy the harmonious blend of spices, butter, and cream that defines this iconic dish.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in hot water for 15 mins

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Mix well and let it marinate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Base Sauce
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Saute for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Build the Makhani Gravy
instructions.text: Add 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste to the pan and cook for 1 minute until aromatic. Pour in the tomato puree, soaked and blended cashews (blend cashews with a little water until smooth), sugar, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil separates.

instructions.title: Finish the Sauce
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (optional, for smoother sauce). Blend the sauce with an immersion blender until smooth, or carefully transfer to a regular blender and blend. Pass through a fine-mesh sieve for an extra silky texture, if desired. Return the smooth sauce to the pan.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the smooth sauce. Stir in the heavy cream and crushed Kasoori Methi. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the richness of the sauce. Adjust salt and sweetness if needed.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice. Enjoy your homemade Classic Murgh Makhani!

tips: For an authentic smoky flavor, you can char the cooked chicken pieces slightly under a broiler or on a grill before adding them to the sauce.
Soaking cashews is crucial for a smooth, rich gravy. If you dont have cashews, almonds or even a tablespoon of almond flour can be a substitute.

Always cook the tomato puree thoroughly until the oil separates; this step is key to developing the deep flavor of the makhani sauce and removing any raw tomato taste. Blending and straining the sauce ensures a restaurant-quality smooth texture.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, is a culinary masterpiece that has captivated palates across the globe. This iconic Indian dish, with its luscious, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of marinated chicken, is the epitome of comfort food. Its rich history, complex flavors, and universal appeal make it a perennial favorite in Indian restaurants and home kitchens alike. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality experience in your own home, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about crafting the perfect classic Murgh Makhani.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken is a dish that needs no introduction for lovers of Indian cuisine. Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant, it was reportedly created by accident when chefs mixed leftover tandoori chicken with a tomato-based gravy enriched with butter and cream. The result was an instant sensation that quickly spread across India and then the world.

Murgh Makhani literally translates to "chicken with butter," and this name perfectly encapsulates the essence of the dish. It features succulent pieces of boneless chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices, then grilled or pan-fried until lightly charred. These flavorful chicken pieces are then simmered in a velvety smooth sauce made from tomatoes, butter, cream, cashews, and a blend of aromatic spices like garam masala and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). The sauce is characterized by its balanced sweet, tangy, and savory profile, with a subtle underlying warmth rather than intense heat. It’s the perfect harmony of textures and tastes, making Butter Chicken a universally beloved dish.

## Ingredients for Classic Butter Chicken

Crafting authentic Murgh Makhani requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients. Here’s a breakdown of what you’ll need:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   **Chicken:** 1.5-2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces. Thighs are often preferred for their tenderness.
*   **Yogurt:** 1/2 cup plain full-fat yogurt (Greek yogurt can be used, but thin it slightly).
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon (freshly made is best).
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color), 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon ground cumin, 1 teaspoon ground coriander, 1/2 teaspoon salt.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 1 tablespoon (optional, for tenderizing and flavor).

**For the Rich Makhani Sauce:**
*   **Butter:** 4-6 tablespoons unsalted butter. This is Murgh Makhani, after all!
*   **Onion:** 1 medium onion, finely chopped or pureed.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed.
*   **Cashews:** 1/4 cup raw cashews, soaked in hot water for 15-20 minutes (for creaminess and thickness).
*   **Cream:** 1/2 cup heavy cream or whipping cream.
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, 1 tablespoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed.
*   **Sweetener:** 1-2 teaspoons sugar or honey, to balance the acidity of the tomatoes.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Garnish:** Fresh cilantro, chopped.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Murgh Makhani

Making authentic Butter Chicken at home is a rewarding experience. Follow these steps for a truly delicious outcome:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure the chicken is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or ideally overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate and the chicken to tenderize.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) or a grill pan/skillet over medium-high heat. If using an oven, arrange the marinated chicken on a baking sheet lined with foil and sprayed with oil. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked through and slightly charred. If using a skillet, add a little oil and cook the chicken in batches until nicely browned and cooked. Set the cooked chicken aside.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped or pureed onion and sauté until translucent and softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Add Tomatoes and Cashews:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews (drained), and red chili powder. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the sauce has thickened slightly. This allows the flavors to meld.

5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the cooked tomato and cashew mixture to a blender. Blend until absolutely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky sauce, you can strain it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot, discarding any fibrous bits.

6.  **Finish the Sauce:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot. Add the remaining 2-4 tablespoons of butter, garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, sugar/honey, and salt to taste. Stir well and bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.

7.  **Combine and Serve:** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering sauce. Stir gently to coat the chicken completely. Cook for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce. Stir in the heavy cream just before serving, warming it through but not boiling, as boiling can cause the cream to separate.

8.  **Garnish:** Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that perfect restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is truly ideal.
*   **Charred Chicken for Depth:** Lightly charring the chicken (either under a broiler, on a grill, or in a hot pan) before adding it to the sauce mimics the flavor profile of tandoori chicken and adds a wonderful smoky depth that is crucial for authentic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Silky Smooth Sauce:** Blend your sauce thoroughly! For the ultimate restaurant-style texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve. This removes any tiny bits of onion or cashew, resulting in an incredibly smooth and luxurious gravy.
*   **Kasoori Methi (Fenugreek Leaves):** This spice is non-negotiable for authentic flavor. Gently toast the dried leaves in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing them between your palms and adding them to the sauce. This releases their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** Taste and adjust! The sweetness from sugar/honey balances the acidity of the tomatoes. Don't be afraid to adjust the salt, sugar, or even a tiny squeeze of lemon juice at the end to achieve your perfect flavor balance.
*   **Good Quality Tomatoes:** Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes (like San Marzano) or very ripe fresh tomatoes for the best flavor foundation.

## Serving Suggestions for Butter Chicken

No matter how you serve it, Butter Chicken is always a crowd-pleaser and a truly versatile dish. It's designed to be the star of your meal.

*   **Naan Bread:** The classic pairing! Garlic naan, butter naan, or plain naan are perfect for scooping up every last bit of that delicious sauce.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera (cumin) rice provides a wonderful contrast and absorbs the rich gravy beautifully.
*   **Roti or Chapati:** For a lighter option, serve with warm whole wheat rotis or chapatis.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt dip with cucumber or mint can be a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A simple side salad of sliced red onion, cucumber, and tomatoes with a squeeze of lime juice can add freshness.
*   **Garnish:** Always finish with a generous sprinkle of fresh cilantro and, if you're feeling indulgent, a swirl of extra cream or a small dollop of butter.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is perfect as is, don't hesitate to experiment with these variations to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter "Chicken":** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) for Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, chickpeas, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas for equally delicious meat-free versions.
*   **Healthier Options:** To reduce the richness, you can use less butter and cream. Substitute heavy cream with half-and-half, evaporated milk, or even a cashew-almond milk blend for a lighter touch. Baking or air-frying the chicken also reduces oil.
*   **Spice Level Adjustments:** For more heat, add finely chopped green chilies to the sauce base along with the ginger-garlic paste, or increase the amount of red chili powder. For milder versions, reduce the chili powder or use paprika for color without the heat.
*   **Nut-Free Version:** If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews. To maintain creaminess and thickness, increase the amount of heavy cream, or use soaked and blended melon seeds or sunflower seeds as an alternative.
*   **Dairy-Free Version:** Use a plant-based yogurt for the marinade (e.g., coconut or almond yogurt). For the sauce, use a high-quality plant-based butter and full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of dairy cream. Ensure your garam masala is dairy-free.

No matter how you choose to make it, classic Murgh Makhani is a celebration of flavor and a testament to the richness of Indian cuisine. Enjoy the process of creating this beloved dish, and savor every creamy, comforting bite!
